title: Hash Maps: Lightning-Fast Lookups
subtitle: Find anything by name in constant time

sections.heading: The superpower
sections.body: A **hash map** (or **object** or **Map** in JS) stores key-value pairs. The magic: looking up a value by its key is **O(1)** — almost instant — no matter how many keys you have.

sections.title: A filing cabinet with labeled drawers
sections.body: Imagine a cabinet where every drawer has a unique name on it. You don't search drawer by drawer — you walk straight to 'TaxReturns2024' and pull it open. That's how a hash map feels.

sections.heading: Classic use: count things
sections.body: Hash maps are perfect for counting. Walk through a list, use each item as a key, increment its value. Need to find duplicates, frequencies, or anagrams? Reach for a hash map.

sections.prompt: Write `firstDuplicate(arr)` that returns the first value that appears twice, or -1 if none.
hints: Use a Set to remember which values you've already seen.
Walk through the array once. The first repeat is your answer.

questions.question: Average time to look up a value by key in a hash map?
questions.explanation: Hash maps offer average O(1) lookups — that's their defining feature.
